other_observers: Joey Kellner; Discovered by Joey Kellner
description: A tanager, mostly dusky yellow with charcoal grayish wings, appearing darker than the rest of the bird. Thin wing bar created by paler tips to greater coverts only. Pale edges on tertials. The bird had a greenish-yellow back which isolated the folded wings by color, unlike Western Tanager. Bill was smaller than Summer Tanager and dull.Behaviors: Sitting and slowly foraging, while eluding 2 other observers.Call: none heardPlumage: 1st fall male possibly adult female
